concept.shortdesc: Park the lift truck in a designated parking area on a level surface. Avoid parking

conbody.p: Park the lift truck in a designated parking area on a level surface. Avoid parking on slopes or near doorways.
Lower the forks completely to the floor. Tilt the mast slightly forward so the fork tips touch the ground.
Place the directional control lever in NEUTRAL. Apply the parking brake.
Turn the engine off and remove the ignition key.
Do not park on inclines. If parking on an incline is unavoidable, use wheel chocks in addition to the parking brake.
After parking, complete the post-operation inspection and record any defects in the logbook.
Page 37 of 500 | CD100S-7 / CD120S-7 / CD140S-7 / CD160S-7 Operation & Maintenance Manual | DL06P Tier-4
Always follow all safety precautions when operating or maintaining this equipment.
Ensure all operators are properly trained and certified before operating the lift truck.
Regular maintenance is essential to safe and efficient operation of the lift truck.
Contact your local CROWN dealer for parts, service, and technical support.
Keep this manual with the lift truck at all times for reference by operators and technicians.
Record all service and maintenance activities in the maintenance logbook.
Only use genuine CROWN replacement parts to maintain warranty and performance.
